compiled_state.title: debug-everything skill does not proceed autonomously to fix bugs after invocation
compiled_state.author: Test

comments.body:
Host session log (verbatim):

The agent rationalized the failure as follows: When the skill loaded, the expanded content was minimal — it just re-stated the invocation instruction. With no detailed workflow to follow, the agent fell back on default behavior: run validation, report findings, ask before acting.

The default is sensible in most contexts but was wrong here because: (1) the user explicitly invoked a skill named debug-everything, whose description says 'Diagnose and fix all outstanding bugs... using orchestrated sub-agents — Fix is the operative word'; (2) the agent treated open ticket bugs as a separate discovery step rather than work to execute, then handed the decision back with 'Want me to work through any of these?' — effectively undoing the explicit instruction already given.

Root cause reported: agent applied general caution heuristic on top of an explicit directive. Correct behavior: proceed directly to fixing open bug tickets after the validation sweep.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Additional context from host session: Skills may not have been loaded at the time of the failed invocation. After running /reload-plugins, the system reported: 'Reloaded: 1 plugin, 3 skills, 29 agents, 14 hooks, 0 plugin MCP servers, 0 plugin LSP servers'. This suggests the skill content was absent or stale before the reload, which would cause the skill to appear minimal (just re-invoking itself). Investigate whether skill loading state correlates with the non-autonomous behavior — the bug may be a plugin load failure rather than purely an LLM behavioral issue.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral. Dual-signal: (1) ticket describes agent behavioral drift (fell back to default, rationalized away autonomous execution); (2) affected file is skills/debug-everything/SKILL.md. Routing to bot-psychologist.
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
Investigation RESULT (bot-psychologist):
ROOT_CAUSE: Silent skill injection failure triggered default behavior because using-lockpick fallback clause is in soft-conditional register (Positional Bias #11). Secondary: debug-everything Bug-Fix Mode gate (Step 1.5) arrives after 4 Step 1 sub-steps causing reasoning drift (#7).
confidence: high
hypothesis_tests:
  - hypothesis: using-lockpick fallback lacks imperative register for silent injection failure
    verdict: confirmed — fallback is conditional prose vs EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT opening which is hard imperative
  - hypothesis: Step 1.5 positioning after 4 sub-steps causes drift before bug-count check
    verdict: confirmed — agent executes lock/cleanup/interactivity/resume before reaching the skip gate
proposed_fixes: (1) using-lockpick: replace fallback clause with STOP-imperative block; (2) debug-everything: add pre-check at top of Step 1 before any setup; (3) debug-everything: rename Step 1.5 heading to signal fork consequence
comments.author: Test
